hwidBetaWarningDesc: HWID tracking is currently in beta version and works only with happ and v2raytun clients. Other clients may not support HWID registration.
secAlertSsl: This connection is not secure. Please avoid entering sensitive information until TLS is activated for data protection.
secAlertConf: Certain settings are vulnerable to attacks. It is recommended to reinforce security protocols to prevent potential breaches.
secAlertSSL: Panel lacks secure connection. Please install TLS certificate for data protection.
secAlertPanelPort: Panel default port is vulnerable. Please configure a random or specific port.
secAlertPanelURI: Panel default URI path is insecure. Please configure a complex URI path.
secAlertSubURI: Subscription default URI path is insecure. Please configure a complex URI path.
secAlertSubJsonURI: Subscription JSON default URI path is insecure. Please configure a complex URI path.

pages.login.title: Welcome
pages.login.loginAgain: Your session has expired, please log in again
pages.login.toasts.invalidFormData: The Input data format is invalid.
pages.login.toasts.wrongUsernameOrPasswo: Invalid username or password or two-factor code.
pages.login.toasts.needTwoFactor: Enter the authentication code, or submit again to receive it via Telegram if enabled.
pages.login.toasts.twoFactorTelegramSent: A login code was sent to your Telegram admin chat(s).
pages.login.toasts.successLogin: You have successfully logged into your account.
pages.index.title: Overview
pages.index.xraySwitchClick: Choose the version you want to switch to.
pages.index.xraySwitchClickDesk: Choose carefully, as older versions may not be compatible with current configurations.
pages.index.xrayErrorPopoverTitle: An error occurred while running Xray
pages.index.systemLoadDesc: System load average for the past 1, 5, and 15 minutes
pages.index.xraySwitchVersionDialog: Do you really want to change the Xray version?
pages.index.xraySwitchVersionDialogDesc: This will change the Xray version to #version#.
pages.index.geofileUpdateDialog: Do you really want to update the geofile?
pages.index.geofileUploadDialog: Do you really want to upload this geofile?
pages.index.geofileRollbackDialog: Do you really want to rollback this geofile?
pages.index.geofileNodePartial: Completed locally, but some nodes failed
pages.index.dontRefresh: Installation is in progress, please do not refresh this page
pages.index.exportDatabaseDesc: Click to download a SQL dump file containing a backup of your current database to your device.
pages.index.importDatabaseDesc: Click to select and upload a SQL dump file (.sql) from your device to restore your database from a backup.
pages.index.importDatabaseSuccess: The database has been successfully imported.
pages.index.importDatabaseError: An error occurred while importing the database.
pages.index.readDatabaseError: An error occurred while reading the database.
pages.index.getDatabaseError: An error occurred while retrieving the database.
pages.index.getConfigError: An error occurred while retrieving the config file.
pages.index.xrayLocalNotRunningMulti: Not running on panel (normal in node mode)
pages.index.nodesCoresNoEnabled: No enabled nodes — add or enable nodes to see core status on workers.
pages.index.telemtLocalIdleMulti: Telemt on the panel host is not used in multi-node mode
pages.index.panelProcessDesc: The dashboard server process: CPU topology, reported clock, goroutines, and memory held by the panel binary.
pages.index.dashboardCustomizeDesc: Turn sections on or off. At least one block must stay visible.
pages.index.usersOnlineHint: Distinct client emails with active usage or presence (same source as the client list / traffic collection).
pages.index.hwidUserAgentDashboardHint: Overall share of registered devices across all clients.
pages.inbounds.title: Inbounds
pages.inbounds.editInboundHint: Protocol cannot be changed for an existing inbound.
pages.inbounds.deleteInboundContent: Are you sure you want to delete inbound?
pages.inbounds.deleteClientContent: Are you sure you want to delete client?
pages.inbounds.resetTrafficContent: Are you sure you want to reset traffic?
pages.inbounds.monitorDesc: Leave blank to listen on all IPs
pages.inbounds.noRecommendKeepDefault: It is recommended to keep the default
pages.inbounds.clickOnQRcode: Click on QR Code to Copy
pages.inbounds.cloneInboundContent: All settings of this inbound, except Port, Listening IP, and Clients, will be applied to the clone.
pages.inbounds.resetAllTrafficContent: Are you sure you want to reset the traffic of all inbounds?
pages.inbounds.resetInboundClientTraffic: Are you sure you want to reset the traffic of this inbound's clients?
pages.inbounds.resetAllClientTrafficCont: Are you sure you want to reset the traffic of all clients?
pages.inbounds.delDepletedClientsContent: Are you sure you want to delete all the depleted clients?
pages.inbounds.emailDesc: Please provide a unique email address.
pages.inbounds.IPLimitDesc: Disables inbound if the count exceeds the set value. (0 = disable)
pages.inbounds.IPLimitlogDesc: The IPs history log. (to enable inbound after disabling, clear the log)
pages.inbounds.generateSelfSignedTlsHint: Uses Server name (SNI) as the certificate CN when set, with that name in SAN. Clears certificate file paths and fills the PEM fields below. Clients must trust this certificate or use allow insecure.
pages.inbounds.telegramDesc: Please provide Telegram Chat ID. (use '/id' command in the bot) or (@userinfobot)
pages.inbounds.subscriptionDesc: To find your subscription URL, navigate to the 'Details'. Additionally, you can use the same name for several clients.
pages.inbounds.periodicTrafficResetDesc: Automatically reset traffic counter at specified intervals
pages.inbounds.hysteriaAuthDesc: Hysteria2 shared secret (mapped to auth in Xray).
pages.inbounds.addInboundEditorFollowUp: A full in-panel editor (protocol, stream, security, clients) is planned in a follow-up. This view lists inbounds and supports delete only.
pages.inbounds.addInboundApiBlurb: Create a new inbound with a POST and JSON body (protocol, port, tag, streamSettings, sniffing, and related fields — see the reference).
pages.inbounds.filterNoResults: No inbounds match the current filters.
pages.inbounds.addInboundFirstClientHint: Shown in the panel; must be unique among clients. Used as subscription id in many clients.
pages.inbounds.addInboundNodesHint: Only in multi-node mode. Leave all unchecked to assign later.
pages.inbounds.wireguardSettingsHint: Server-side WireGuard (UDP): MTU, private key, tunnel addresses, optional peers (client public keys), noKernelTun, optional workers. Settings JSON is built on the server. There are no panel-managed client entries for this protocol.
pages.inbounds.wireguardPeersEmpty: No peers yet. Add a row for each client public key (optional PSK, allowedIPs).
pages.inbounds.wireguardTransportHint: WireGuard uses UDP on the inbound port. There is no separate TCP/WebSocket `streamSettings` — leave the generated empty `{}` and set MTU, keys, addresses, and peers in Protocol authentication on the next step.
pages.inbounds.helpModalP1: An inbound is a listener on your nodes: protocol, port, stream transport (TCP, WebSocket, gRPC, REALITY, …), and TLS determine how user clients reach the proxy.
pages.inbounds.helpModalP2: Create different inbounds when you need another port, camouflage, or protocol stack. User credentials (UUID, passwords, keys) live on clients that reference these inbounds.
pages.inbounds.helpModalP3: Assign inbounds to worker nodes so configuration is pushed where traffic should terminate. Cloning copies settings to speed up similar profiles (you still pick a unique port / listen IP).
pages.inbounds.helpModalP4: Use filters and sorting for large deployments. From each row’s actions you can reset traffic, maintain clients, or export links—keep TLS and Reality parameters consistent with what your CDN or firewall allows.
pages.inbounds.addInboundNodesEmpty: No nodes configured yet. Inbound will run on the main server.
pages.inbounds.hysteriaTlsHint: QUIC uses TLS like 3x-ui: SNI, ALPN (usually h3), certificate paths (optional — panel default cert is applied if empty).
pages.inbounds.mixedAuthHint: First saved account in settings; panel clients map email local-part to this user when syncing to Xray.
pages.inbounds.mixedStreamHint: Mixed serves HTTP and SOCKS on one port — plain TCP only. TLS/REALITY and WebSocket do not apply here.
pages.inbounds.sectionAuthNone: No protocol-specific secrets on this screen (e.g. VMess uses client UUIDs in settings).
pages.inbounds.shadowsocksStreamHint: Shadowsocks already encrypts payload. The stream is plain TCP or WebSocket (no TLS/REALITY here — that applies to VLESS, VMess, Trojan).
pages.inbounds.stepTelemtAuthDesc: Fake-TLS modes, SNI domain, links, API
pages.inbounds.streamSecurityNoneForSs: Stream security is none (Xray default for this protocol).
pages.inbounds.telemtMetricsPortHint: [server] Prometheus/HTTP metrics listener port; leave empty to omit.
pages.inbounds.telemtModesExplain: tls = Fake-TLS (Telegram `ee…` links). secure = `dd…` prefix. classic = plain MTProto secret. Official reference: github.com/telemt/telemt — config.toml & docs.
pages.inbounds.telemtSettingsHint: Open this wizard step from the left: «Telemt (MTProto)». Here you configure Telemt [general], [general.modes] (Fake-TLS = tls), [censorship] SNI/masking, links and API. Client MTProto secrets are created when users are assigned to this inbound.
pages.inbounds.telemtTlsDomainHint: Fake-TLS fronting hostname written to Telemt [censorship] tls_domain. Optional JSON key censorship.sni is accepted as an alias when importing configs.
pages.inbounds.telemtTomlPreviewHint: Generated Telemt config.toml (same as deployed to the node or local data/telemt on standalone). [access.users] is empty until you save the inbound and assign clients; after that it reflects the database.
pages.inbounds.telemtTransportHint: Telemt does not use Xray `streamSettings`. The process listens on this inbound port with Telemt-specific options on the next step; users and MTProto secrets are created when clients are assigned.
pages.inbounds.telemtUnknownSniActionHin: Telemt [censorship]: how to treat an SNI mismatch. Default (empty) follows Telemt defaults. mask / reject_handshake when you need explicit policy.
pages.inbounds.tlsCertHint: Use certificate file + key file on the server, or paste PEM blocks below.
pages.inbounds.xhttpHint: XHTTP (SplitHTTP) — same fields as 3x-ui / Xray xhttpSettings (request headers, mode, padding, session, uplink).
pages.inbounds.xrayCorePreviewHint: Single inbound object as it is merged into the Xray core config (listen, port, tag, protocol, settings, streamSettings, sniffing). Panel API request format is not shown here.
pages.inbounds.toasts.resetAllClientTraf: All traffic from the client has been reset.
pages.inbounds.toasts.getNewX25519CertEr: Error while obtaining the X25519 certificate.
pages.inbounds.stream.general.name: Name
pages.settings.title: Panel Settings
pages.settings.infoDesc: Every change made here needs to be saved. Please restart the panel to apply changes.
pages.settings.restartPanelDesc: Are you sure you want to restart the panel? If you cannot access the panel after restarting, please view the panel log info on the server.
pages.settings.dockerUpdaterTriggerSucce: Update job was sent to the Docker sidecar. The panel may restart in a moment.
pages.settings.dockerUpdaterTriggerError: Could not trigger the Docker sidecar updater.
pages.settings.panelListeningIPDesc: The IP address for the web panel. (leave blank to listen on all IPs)
pages.settings.panelListeningDomainDesc: The domain name for the web panel. (leave blank to listen on all domains and IPs)
pages.settings.panelPortDesc: The port number for the web panel. (must be an unused port)
pages.settings.publicKeyPathDesc: The public key file path for the web panel. (begins with ‘/‘)
pages.settings.privateKeyPathDesc: The private key file path for the web panel. (begins with ‘/‘)
pages.settings.panelUrlPathDesc: The URI path for the web panel. (begins with ‘/‘ and concludes with ‘/‘)
pages.settings.pageSizeDesc: Define page size for inbounds table. (0 = disable)
pages.settings.datepickerDescription: Scheduled tasks will run based on this calendar.
pages.settings.remarkModelDesc: The first character is the separator in subscription display names. The rest are a sequence of: i = inbound name, e = user email, o = per-client / extra remark, n = node name, p = node host, r = port.
pages.settings.remarkModelOrder: Field order (letters i e o n p r)
pages.settings.remarkModelOrderHint: Reorder with the arrows; add segments with +i … +r. Default -ieo joins inbound, email, and extra with the separator. Invalid letters are ignored when saving.
pages.settings.remarkModelOrderEmpty: No fields in the sequence yet — add letters below.
pages.settings.telegramTokenDesc: The Telegram bot token obtained from '@BotFather'.
pages.settings.telegramProxyDesc: SOCKS5 or HTTP(S) CONNECT, or host:port as SOCKS5. Supports tg://proxy?… and t.me/proxy?… links. MTProxy links with a secret are not supported for the Bot API—use SOCKS/HTTP or self-hosted Bot API in «API server».
pages.settings.telegramAPIServerDesc: The Telegram API server to use. Leave blank to use the default server.
pages.settings.telegramChatIdDesc: The Telegram Admin Chat ID(s). (comma-separated)(get it here @userinfobot) or (use '/id' command in the bot)
pages.settings.telegramNotifyTimeDesc: Choose how often the bot sends periodic reports to admins. Times use the panel time zone (General → Panel time).
pages.settings.tgRunTimeOptionDailyAt: Every day at a set time
pages.settings.tgRunTimeCustomHint: Six fields: second, minute, hour, day, month, weekday. Example: 0 0 8 * * * is every day at 08:00.
pages.settings.tgNotifyBackupDesc: Send a database backup file with a report.
pages.settings.tgNotifyLoginDesc: Get notified about the username, IP address, and time whenever someone attempts to log into your web panel.
pages.settings.sessionMaxAgeDesc: The duration for which you can stay logged in. (unit: minute)
pages.settings.expireTimeDiffDesc: Get notified about expiration date when reaching this threshold. (unit: day)
pages.settings.trafficDiffDesc: Get notified about traffic cap when reaching this threshold. (unit: GB)
pages.settings.tgNotifyCpuDesc: Get notified if CPU load exceeds this threshold. (unit: %)
pages.settings.timeZoneDesc: Scheduled tasks will run based on this time zone.
pages.settings.subJsonEnable: Enable/Disable the JSON subscription endpoint independently.
pages.settings.subTitle: Subscription Title
pages.settings.subListenDesc: The IP address for the subscription service. (leave blank to listen on all IPs)
pages.settings.subPortDesc: The port number for the subscription service. (must be an unused port)
pages.settings.subCertPathDesc: The public key file path for the subscription service. (begins with ‘/‘)
pages.settings.subKeyPathDesc: The private key file path for the subscription service. (begins with ‘/‘)
pages.settings.subPathDesc: The URI path for the subscription service. (begins with ‘/‘ and concludes with ‘/‘)
pages.settings.subDomainDesc: The domain name for the subscription service. (leave blank to listen on all domains and IPs)
pages.settings.subUpdatesDesc: The update intervals of the subscription URL in the client apps. (unit: hour)
pages.settings.subEncryptDesc: The returned content of subscription service will be Base64 encoded.
pages.settings.subShowInfoDesc: The remaining traffic and date will be displayed in the client apps.
pages.settings.subEncryptHappV2RayTunDes: Encrypt subscription links using RSA-4096 for Happ and V2RayTun applications
pages.settings.subOnlyHappV2RayTun: Only include Happ and V2RayTun compatible links
pages.settings.subOnlyHappV2RayTunDesc: Filter subscription to only include links compatible with Happ and V2RayTun (vless, vmess, trojan, shadowsocks)
pages.settings.subHideConfigLinks: Hide configuration links on subscription page
pages.settings.subHideConfigLinksDesc: Hide vless, vmess and other configuration links visually on the subscription page
pages.settings.subShowOnlyHappV2RayTunDe: Display only Happ and V2RayTun application buttons on the subscription page, hiding all others
pages.settings.subProviderIDDesc: Provider identifier for using Happ extended headers (new-url, new-domain, etc.). Usually a UUID. Required for Happ extended features to work.
pages.settings.subProviderIDMethodDesc: Choose how to send Provider ID to clients according to Happ documentation: in URL as query parameter (?providerid=...), in HTTP response header (providerid: ...), in subscription body as comment (#providerid ...), or disabled
pages.settings.subURIDesc: The URI path of the subscription URL for use behind proxies.
pages.settings.externalTrafficInformEnab: Inform external API on every traffic update.
pages.settings.externalTrafficInformURID: Traffic updates are sent to this URI.
pages.settings.fragmentDesc: Enable fragmentation for TLS hello packet.
pages.settings.muxDesc: Transmit multiple independent data streams within an established data stream.
pages.settings.multiNodeModeDesc: Enable distributed architecture with separate worker nodes. When enabled, XRAY Core runs on nodes instead of locally.
pages.settings.multiNodeModePoint1: XRAY Core will not run locally
pages.settings.multiNodeModePoint2: Configurations will be sent to worker nodes
pages.settings.multiNodeModePoint3: You need to assign inbounds to nodes
pages.settings.enableMultiNodeModeConfir: Enabling multi-node mode will stop local XRAY Core. Make sure you have configured worker nodes before enabling this mode. Continue?
pages.settings.directDesc: Directly establishes connections with domains or IP ranges of a specific country.
pages.settings.languageDesc: Applies to this browser only. The choice is saved in local storage on your device.
pages.settings.panelThemeDesc: Choose accent palette and background. The choice is saved in this browser only.
pages.settings.grafanaEnableDesc: When enabled, all logs (including xray and node logs) will be sent to Grafana Loki, and local file logging will be disabled. All log levels will be enabled.
pages.settings.grafanaDashboardDesc: Download ready-to-use Grafana dashboard JSON file
pages.settings.grafanaMetricsEndpointDes:
Metrics are available at: /panel/metrics

To configure Grafana/Prometheus scraping:
1. Add a Prometheus data source in Grafana pointing to your SharX panel
2. Configure scrape job with target: http://your-panel-host:port/panel/metrics
3. Import the downloaded dashboard JSON file

Example Prometheus scrape config:
- job_name: 'sharx'
  scrape_interval: 30s
  static_configs:
    - targets: ['your-panel-host:port']
      metrics_path: '/panel/metrics'
pages.settings.grafanaIntegrationEnabled:
When Grafana integration is enabled:
• All logs (x-ui, xray, and node logs) will be sent to Loki
• Local file logging will be disabled
• All log levels (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR) will be enabled
• Metrics will be exported in Prometheus format
• Metrics can be scraped from /panel/metrics endpoint
pages.settings.remarkModelLegendIntro: The first character is the separator in subscription display names. The rest are a sequence of fields: i = inbound name, e = user email, o = per-client remark, n = node name, p = node host, r = port.
pages.settings.panelLogLevelDesc: Log verbosity for the panel process (when Grafana Loki is disabled).
pages.settings.enableIPv6Desc: When disabled, dashboard public IPv6 detection is turned off and IPv6 is hidden.
pages.settings.envOnlyHint: Effective values for these options are usually set via environment variables; shown values are what the API reports.
pages.settings.grafanaVictoriaMetricsUrl: Remote write / import endpoint for metrics.
pages.settings.subPageURIDesc: Override the base URL of the public subscription page (/panel/sub/) behind a reverse proxy. Leave blank to auto-detect.
pages.settings.subJsonPathDesc: The URI path for the JSON subscription endpoint. (begins with '/' and ends with '/')
pages.settings.subJsonURIDesc: Override the base URL of the JSON subscription endpoint behind a reverse proxy. Leave blank to auto-detect.
pages.settings.nodeStatsIntervalDesc: How often the panel collects traffic statistics from worker nodes, in seconds.
pages.settings.nodeHealthIntervalDesc: How often the panel checks if worker nodes are online, in seconds.
pages.settings.nodeHealthDegradedInterva: How often the panel polls a node that was recently unreachable, in seconds.
pages.settings.envOnlyNetworkTitle: Network is configured via environment variables
pages.settings.envOnlyNetworkDesc: Port, path, domain and TLS cert/key for the subscription endpoint are read from XUI_SUB_PORT / XUI_SUB_PATH / XUI_SUB_DOMAIN / XUI_SUB_CERT_FILE / XUI_SUB_KEY_FILE. Edit .env and restart the panel.
pages.settings.dockerUpdaterProgressHint: Please wait while update is being applied.
pages.settings.sections.subAdvancedJsonH: Large JSON snippets for clients. Expand only when you need them.
pages.settings.sections.subpageBuilderHi: Branding, blocks, response headers, app settings and JSON templates — all in one live-preview builder for /panel/sub/?id=…
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.title: Subscription Headers
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.profi: Subscription name shown in the application (max 25 chars for Happ)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.subsc: Traffic and expiry information: upload=X; download=Y; total=Z; expire=T
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.suppo: Support page link (displayed as icon on the right)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.profi: Subscription website link (displayed as icon on the left)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.annou: Announcement text for users (max 200 chars, supports base64)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.newDo: New domain for subscription without changing full URL (requires Provider ID)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.serve: Additional server description (max 30 chars, supports base64)
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.subEx: Link for 'Renew' button in expiry notification
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.subIn: Button text in information block (max 25 chars)
Link opened when clicking button in information block
pages.settings.subscriptionHeaders.updat: Force subscription update on every app open (true)
pages.settings.migration.title: Migration
pages.settings.migration.infoDesc: Migrate data from old SQLite database (x-ui.db) to new PostgreSQL database. Clients will be extracted from JSON fields and converted to new structure.
pages.settings.migration.preview.panelSe: The following panel settings from the old database will be applied after migration. Remember them, as the container will be restarted with new settings.
pages.settings.migration.panelRestartWar: Warning: After successful migration, the Docker container will be automatically restarted to apply new settings. Please remember the new settings (port, path, etc.) as you may need them to reconnect.
pages.settings.migration.result.panelRes: Container has been restarted with the new settings. If you cannot access the panel, check the new port and path settings.
pages.settings.migration.error.invalidFi: Invalid file type. Please select a .db file
This will migrate data from SQLite to PostgreSQL. Existing data may be updated. Continue?
pages.settings.security.twoFactorEnableD: Adds an additional layer of authentication to provide more security.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorModalSt: To set up two-factor authentication, perform a few steps:
pages.settings.security.twoFactorModalFi: 1. Scan this QR code in the app for authentication or copy the token near the QR code and paste it into the app
pages.settings.security.twoFactorModalSe: 2. Enter the code from the app
pages.settings.security.twoFactorModalRe: Enter the code from the application to remove two-factor authentication.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorModalCh: Enter the code from the application to change administrator credentials.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorModalSe: Two-factor authentication has been successfully established
pages.settings.security.twoFactorModalDe: Two-factor authentication has been successfully deleted
pages.settings.security.twoFactorBeginUn: You must be logged in to set up two-factor authentication.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorBeginEr: Could not start authenticator setup. Try again.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorComplet: Could not complete authenticator setup. Start over from the QR step.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorTelegra: After a correct password, sends the same 6-digit code to admin chat IDs (Telegram settings) so you can sign in without an authenticator app. Requires the bot enabled and running.
pages.settings.security.twoFactorDisable: Disable two-factor authentication? You can set it up again afterward.
pages.settings.security.apiTokenHint: Use Bearer tokens for scripts and API clients without browser cookies. Store them safely; the full value is shown only once when created.
pages.settings.security.apiTokenConfirmR: Revoke this token? Scripts and clients using it will stop working.
pages.settings.security.apiTokenModalHin: Copy it now. You will not be able to see the full token again.
pages.settings.toasts.getSettings: An error occurred while retrieving parameters.
pages.settings.toasts.modifyUserError: An error occurred while changing administrator credentials.
pages.settings.toasts.modifyUser: You have successfully changed the credentials of the administrator.
pages.settings.toasts.originalUserPassIn: The current username or password is invalid
pages.settings.toasts.userPassMustBeNotE: The new username and password is empty
pages.settings.subCustomize.themeDesc: Select a visual theme for the subscription page
pages.settings.subCustomize.logoUrlDesc: URL of the logo image (32x32 or 64x64 pixels)
pages.settings.subCustomize.brandTextDes: Text to display next to the logo
pages.xray.title: Xray Configs
pages.xray.nodeModeInfo: In node mode, core settings are applied through configuration profiles. Save settings as a profile to apply them to nodes.
pages.xray.restartError: There was an error when rebooting the Xray.
pages.xray.stopError: There was an error when stopping the Xray.
pages.xray.generalConfigsDesc: These options will determine general adjustments.
pages.xray.logConfigsDesc: Logs may affect your server's efficiency. It is recommended to enable it wisely only in case of your needs
pages.xray.blockConfigsDesc: These options will block traffic based on specific requested protocols and websites.
pages.xray.blockConnectionsConfigsDesc: These options will block traffic based on the specific requested country.
pages.xray.directConnectionsConfigsDesc: A direct connection ensures that specific traffic is not routed through another server.
pages.xray.ipv4RoutingDesc: These options will route traffic based on a specific destination via IPv4.
pages.xray.warpRoutingDesc: These options will route traffic based on a specific destination via WARP.
pages.xray.TemplateDesc: This JSON is merged into the Xray process. In node mode, also manage profiles to push configs to workers.
pages.xray.TemplateDescStandalone: Edit the configuration JSON and use Save & apply — settings go to the local Xray core. Stand-alone mode has no separate profile to publish to nodes.
pages.xray.runtimeViewStandalone: Read-only: JSON currently loaded by the core on this server.
pages.xray.sliceEditorHintStandalone: Edit this section as JSON or use the steps above. When you save, the template is applied to the local core.
pages.xray.FreedomStrategyDesc: Set the output strategy for the network in the Freedom Protocol.
pages.xray.RoutingStrategyDesc: Set the overall traffic routing strategy for resolving all requests.
pages.xray.RoutingsDesc: The priority of each rule is important!
pages.xray.logLevelDesc: The log level for error logs, indicating the information that needs to be recorded.
pages.xray.accessLogDesc: The file path for the access log. The special value 'none' disabled access logs
pages.xray.errorLogDesc: The file path for the error log. The special value 'none' disabled error logs
pages.xray.dnsLogDesc: Whether to enable DNS query logs
pages.xray.maskAddressDesc: IP address mask, when enabled, will automatically replace the IP address that appears in the log.
pages.xray.statsInboundUplinkDesc: Enables the statistics collection for upstream traffic of all inbound proxies.
pages.xray.statsInboundDownlinkDesc: Enables the statistics collection for downstream traffic of all inbound proxies.
pages.xray.statsOutboundUplinkDesc: Enables the statistics collection for upstream traffic of all outbound proxies.
pages.xray.statsOutboundDownlinkDesc: Enables the statistics collection for downstream traffic of all outbound proxies.
pages.xray.simpleSectionHint: Common options without editing JSON. Use the menu for routing (balancers), DNS, and other sections.
pages.xray.accessLogToFile: Write access log to a file
pages.xray.simpleJsonFooter: Use the menu to open routing (balancers), DNS, or the full JSON template.
pages.xray.runtimeView: Read-only: JSON currently applied. In node mode, edit the template or profiles, then apply to nodes as usual.
pages.xray.sliceEditorHint: Edit this section as JSON, or use the top steps for a guided layout.
pages.xray.inboundsTemplateHint: User-facing inbounds are usually managed under Inbounds. This template slice is for defaults and the API inbound.
pages.xray.geoSectionDesc: Manage geoip.dat and geosite.dat: upload, rollback, or download by URL.
pages.xray.resetServerTemplateConfirm: Reset the server routing template to the default? Changes apply after you save the Xray config.
pages.xray.simpleApiSectionHint: Handler / Logger / Stats must match the dokodemo-door inbound tag used for the panel.
pages.xray.simpleApiTagDesc: Same as the API inbound `tag` (e.g. api).
pages.xray.simplePolicySectionHint: Toggles for common `policy.levels.0` and `policy.system` stat switches.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.logLoglevel: Stderr log verbosity: debug, info, warning, error, none. Production typical: warning.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.logError: Error log file path, or "none" to disable file error logging.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.logAccess: Access log file path, or "none" to disable access logs.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.logDnsLog: If true, DNS queries are logged (more disk I/O).
pages.xray.jsonSchema.logMaskAddress: Mask IPs in logs for privacy. Empty string disables masking.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.logBlockDesc: Core logging: level, file paths, DNS log toggle, optional address masking.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.apiBlockDesc: gRPC API via a dedicated inbound (often protocol "tunnel"). Remote control and stats.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.apiTag: Tag of the inbound serving the API; routing rules reference it via inboundTag.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.apiServices: gRPC services: HandlerService, LoggerService, StatsService, RoutingService, ObservatoryService, …
pages.xray.jsonSchema.statsBlockDesc: Stats section placeholder; counters are enabled in policy and read via StatsService / metrics.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.policyStatsUserUpl: Collect per-user uplink stats at this policy level.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.policyStatsUserDow: Collect per-user downlink stats at this policy level.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.policyHandshake: Handshake timeout in seconds for connections at this level.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.policyBufferSize: Buffer size hint for this level (bytes).
pages.xray.jsonSchema.policyBlockDesc: Connection policies: user levels (e.g. "0") and global stats toggles.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.policyLevels: Map of level id (string, often "0") to limits and stats flags.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.policySystem: Global statistics switches for all inbounds and outbounds.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRuleDesc: One routing rule; first match wins. type "field" matches domain, IP, tags, etc.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRuleDomain: Domain list: plain, regexp:, domain:, full:, geosite:…
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRulePort: Destination port or range as string, e.g. "443" or "1000-2000".
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRuleUser: Inbound user emails/ids this rule applies to.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRuleInbound: Restrict rule to these inbound tag(s).
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRuleOutboun: Send matching traffic to this outbound tag (or use balancerTag).
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRuleBalance: Send matching traffic to this load balancer.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRuleProtoco: L7 sniffing result: bittorrent, http, tls, …
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRuleDomainM: Domain list algorithm: hybrid, linear, mph.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingDomainStrat: How to resolve domains for routing: AsIs, IPIfNonMatch, IPOnDemand, …
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingRules: Ordered rules; evaluation stops at the first match.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.routingBalancers: Load balancers referenced by balancerTag in rules.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.metricsTag: Tag for the internal metrics path.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundSettings: Per-protocol inbound settings. VMess/VLESS: clients, id, alterId/flow. Trojan: passwords. SS: method, password. WG: secretKey, peers. HTTP/SOCKS: accounts. dokodemo-door: address, port. tunnel (API): address.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundSettingsAdd: dokodemo-door target or tunnel forward address (often 127.0.0.1 for local API).
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundSettingsPor: Target port for dokodemo-door and similar.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundSettingsNet: tcp, udp, tcp,udp — for dokodemo-door and similar.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundSettingsCli: User entries for VMess, VLESS, Trojan, …
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundSettingsFal: TCP fallbacks when using TLS/SNI multiplexing.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.outboundSettings: Per-protocol outbound: VLESS/VMess — vnext; Trojan/SS/HTTP — servers; WG — secretKey, address, peers; freedom — domainStrategy, redirect, noises; blackhole — response; dns; loopback — inboundTag.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.outFreedomDomainSt: freedom only: AsIs, UseIP, UseIPv4, UseIPv6, …
pages.xray.jsonSchema.outVnext: Remote VMess/VLESS endpoints and user objects.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.outServers: Trojan, Shadowsocks, HTTP outbound server list.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.outLoopbackInbound: loopback only: inbound tag whose traffic is re-injected into routing.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.outboundSendThroug: Optional source IP for outbound sockets.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.outboundMux: Mux/SMUX: { enabled, concurrency, … } to combine streams.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundPort: Listen port; some protocols may use 0 or omit.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundAllocate: Port allocation: { strategy: always, refresh, concurrency }.
pages.xray.jsonSchema.inboundsRoot: Inbound array: users, API tunnel, WireGuard, …
pages.xray.jsonSchema.root: Full Xray config: log, api, dns, routing, policy, stats, metrics, inbounds, outbounds. Extra keys allowed.
pages.xray.stepDesc.general: Quick options and log paths without editing full JSON
pages.xray.stepDesc.additionalTopLevel: Additional top-level keys in this template
pages.xray.outbound.wireguardHint: Edit WireGuard settings as JSON (peers, keys, MTU).
pages.xray.outbound.genericProtocolHint: Protocol “{{protocol}}”: adjust settings JSON; add streamSettings if needed.
pages.xray.outbound.streamSettingsJsonHi: tcpSettings, wsSettings, grpcSettings, and other fields. Use when something is not covered by the fields above.
pages.xray.balancer.balancerDesc: It is not possible to use balancerTag and outboundTag at the same time. If used at the same time, only outboundTag will work.
pages.xray.dns.tagDesc: This tag will be available as an Inbound tag in routing rules.
pages.xray.dns.clientIpDesc: Used to notify the server of the specified IP location during DNS queries
pages.xray.dns.disableFallbackIfMatchDes: Disables fallback DNS queries when the matching domain list of the DNS server is hit
pages.xray.dns.enableParallelQueryDesc: Enable parallel DNS queries to multiple servers for faster resolution
pages.xray.dns.strategyDesc: Overall strategy to resolve domain names
pages.xray.dns.useSystemHostsDesc: Use the hosts file from an installed system
pages.xray.dnsBuilder.enableCustom: Use custom DNS object in template
pages.xray.dnsBuilder.hostsHint: Hosts map (JSON object: hostname → IP or array of IPs)
pages.xray.dnsBuilder.disabledHint: DNS is null; the node uses system/default resolvers unless set elsewhere.
pages.xray.routingBuilder.advancedOnly: This routing uses balancers or rules the visual editor cannot represent. Edit as JSON, or remove balancers/complex rules to use the simple rule list.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.title: Core Config Profiles
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.deleteProfi: Are you sure you want to delete profile
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.name: Name
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.configJsonH: Full Xray configuration including routing, dns, log, policy, stats, inbounds, outbounds
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.resetToDefa: Are you sure you want to reset profile
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.coreConfigP: Select Xray core configuration profile for this outbound. If not selected, default profile will be used.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.noProfilesA: No profiles available. Create a profile on the 'Core Config Profiles' page.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.assignNodes: Select nodes to assign to this profile. Each node can only be assigned to one profile.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.pageDesc: Templates for Xray on nodes. Assign a profile to each node; edit by section or General.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.multiNodeOf: Enable multi-node mode in panel settings to use core config profiles.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.addHint: Config is copied from the default profile (or the first one). Edit it after creation.
pages.xrayCoreConfigProfiles.deleteProfi: This cannot be undone. Profiles used by outbounds cannot be deleted.
pages.nodes.title: Nodes Management
pages.nodes.name: Name
pages.nodes.saveSecretKeyHint: Set on the worker as SECRET_KEY or SHARX_NODE_SECRET_KEY. Shown only once — store it safely.
pages.nodes.dockerComposeHint: Ready-to-paste docker-compose (PANEL_URL is this panel; change the image if you use another registry).
pages.nodes.legacyAuth: Legacy registration (API key only, no SECRET_KEY)
pages.nodes.saveApiKeyHint: Save this API key on the node (NODE_API_KEY). It will not be shown again in full.
pages.nodes.validUrl: Must be a valid URL (http:// or https://)
pages.nodes.validPort: Port must be a number between 1 and 65535
pages.nodes.duplicateNode: A node with this address and port already exists
pages.nodes.fullUrlHint: Pairing: in the database you can store http://; the panel still uses TLS to the node API. Without a scheme, https:// is used. Legacy without TLS: enable HTTP in the block below.
pages.nodes.composeFirstStepDesc: The panel uses a single shared SECRET_KEY, so this compose file works for every node you add. The stack includes Watchtower on an internal bridge network (no public port): the worker calls it via 127.0.0.1:8081 to pull a new sharxnode image when you use the panel update button. For production, set WATCHTOWER_HTTP_API_TOKEN in a .env next to compose (e.g. openssl rand -hex 24). Deploy the containers first, then come back and click Next.
pages.nodes.copyComposeOnlyHint: The compose snippet already contains PANEL_URL and the panel-wide SECRET_KEY. Paste it into docker-compose.yml on the node host.
pages.nodes.wizardProfileHint: Choose a core config profile for this node, or skip to use the default profile.
pages.nodes.wizardProfileEmpty: No profiles found. You can add them under Xray - Core config profiles.
pages.nodes.registerInProgress: Please wait — connecting to the node.
pages.nodes.registerFlowHint: The node will be created in the panel, then the panel will verify it can reach the node API.
pages.nodes.registerStepDeployHint: SECRET_KEY (or API key in legacy mode) is ready as soon as the node record exists — you can start the container while the check below runs, or after a failed check use Retry when the node is up.
pages.nodes.draftPendingHint: A node was created but the health check did not pass. You can try again, or remove the draft to start over. Editing the name or address clears the draft for a new run.
pages.nodes.copyError: Could not copy. Try again or select and copy the text manually from the browser.
pages.nodes.hostUrlNoPortHint: Typical public URL (port omitted; worker listens on 8080 on the host): {{url}}
pages.nodes.authPairingDescription: The worker is autonomous: run it with SECRET_KEY on the host. The panel uses mTLS and JWT. No /register to the panel.
pages.nodes.editPairingNote: Pairing material is not edited here. Change the address or limit, or remove and re-add the node to rotate keys.
pages.nodes.apiKeyHint: API key configured on the node (NODE_API_KEY environment variable)
pages.nodes.deleteConfirmText: Are you sure you want to delete this node?
pages.nodes.useTlsHint: Enable TLS/HTTPS for API calls to this node
pages.nodes.certPathHint: Path to CA certificate file (optional, for custom CA)
pages.nodes.keyPathHint: Path to private key file (optional, for client certificate)
pages.nodes.insecureTlsHint: ⚠️ Not recommended: Skip TLS certificate verification (insecure)
pages.nodes.trafficLimitGBHint: Set traffic limit in GB (0 = unlimited). When limit is reached, node will be blocked.
pages.nodes.resetTrafficConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset traffic statistics for this node?
pages.nodes.statsHint: Values are updated by the panel on a schedule (the same per-node database counters as elsewhere).
pages.nodes.helpModalP1: Nodes are servers running the SharX worker and Xray. The panel talks to each node API to deliver configs, health-check connectivity, and aggregate statistics.
pages.nodes.helpModalP2: Add a node with the wizard: paste the suggested Docker Compose on the machine, start the container, then register and verify from the panel. Pairing uses a shared SECRET_KEY; legacy mode can use a static API key instead.
pages.nodes.helpModalP3: Assign inbounds and optional core profiles so the right listeners exist on each worker. You can disable a node without deleting it if you need to pause sync temporarily.
pages.nodes.helpModalP4: Online/offline reflects API reachability; Xray state shows whether the core is running on that worker. Use Statistics and Geography for broader traffic and placement context.
pages.nodes.xrayStopError: Failed to stop Xray on node
pages.nodes.restartXrayOnNode: Restart Xray — also starts core if stopped (force reload)
pages.nodes.restartTelemtOnNode: Restart Telemt — also starts sidecars if stopped
pages.nodes.xrayRestartError: Failed to restart Xray on node
pages.nodes.telemtRestartError: Failed to restart Telemt on node
pages.nodes.geography.title: Node geography
pages.nodes.geography.hint: Approximate locations from public IP (panel on startup; each node when the worker starts). Lines connect nodes to the panel.
pages.nodes.geography.legendHint: Blue: panel. Green/gray: node (green when online). Lines: link to the panel. Nearby points are grouped; the badge shows how many.
pages.nodes.geography.panelPending: Panel location not available yet. It is filled after the next successful lookup on panel startup (outbound HTTPS required).
pages.nodes.geography.panZoomHint: Scroll or pinch to zoom; drag to pan. Zoom in close to split a group into separate pins.
pages.nodes.registerStep.create.title: Register in the panel
pages.nodes.registerStep.create.desc: Create the node record and pairing (SECRET_KEY) in the database.
pages.nodes.registerStep.verify.title: Connect to the node
pages.nodes.registerStep.verify.desc: Run a health check from the panel to the node API.
pages.nodes.toasts.assignError: Failed to assign inbound to node
pages.clients.title: Clients Management
pages.clients.deleteConfirmText: Are you sure you want to delete this client?
pages.clients.addClientNotImplemented: Add client functionality will be implemented soon
pages.clients.editClientNotImplemented: Edit client functionality will be implemented soon
pages.clients.addModalResetHint: 0 = off (no automatic traffic reset on schedule)
pages.clients.addModalTgIdHint: 0 or empty = not linked to Telegram
pages.clients.addModalAnnounceHint: Optional. Max 200 characters, displayed in the subscription.
pages.clients.addModalAdvancedProtocolHi: Empty fields use autogenerated UUID, subscription ID, and protocol defaults for chosen inbounds.
pages.clients.addModalCommentTooLong: Comment must be at most 100 characters
pages.clients.addModalAnnounceTooLong: Announcement must be at most 200 characters
pages.clients.subIdInFormHint: Sub ID can be changed in the subscription field below.
pages.clients.customUuidHint: RFC 4122 format. Leave empty to auto-generate on the server.
pages.clients.createdWithUuidCopied: Client created. UUID copied to clipboard.
pages.clients.maxHwidDesc: Set 0 for unlimited devices. If a new device connects and the limit is reached, the connection will be blocked.
pages.clients.registeredHwidsDesc: List of hardware IDs (devices) that have connected to this client. Only active devices count towards the limit.
pages.clients.hwidUserAgentShareHint: Share of registered devices by User-Agent string.
pages.clients.confirmDeleteHwid: Are you sure you want to delete this device? This will allow a new device to connect if the limit is not reached.
pages.clients.clearHwidContent: Are you sure you want to clear all HWIDs for
pages.clients.clearAllHwidsContent: Are you sure you want to clear all HWIDs for all clients?
pages.clients.setHwidLimitAllContent: Enter the maximum number of devices allowed for all clients:
pages.clients.filterNoResults: No clients match the current filters.
pages.clients.bulkConfirmDelete: Permanently delete {{count}} clients? This cannot be undone.
pages.clients.cardHwidTooltip: HWID restriction is enabled for this client
pages.clients.keysEmptyNoInbounds: This client has no inbounds assigned. Assign inbounds when editing the client, then open Keys again.
pages.clients.keysEmptyCannotBuild: No share links could be built for the assigned inbounds (unsupported protocol, missing password or UUID for this protocol, or panel host/domain settings). Check the inbound type and Settings → subscription domain if links use the panel host.
pages.clients.uuidAfterCreate: Assigned by the server after the client is created.
pages.clients.statsHint: Live snapshot from each worker’s /stats (reset=false). Upload/Download are for the current Xray stat window on that node. “Online” uses the worker’s online client list.
pages.clients.statsEmptyNodes: No worker nodes with inbounds — add nodes or assign inbounds.
pages.clients.statsRowChartHint: Click an email to open a per-user traffic chart by node.
pages.clients.helpModalP1: Clients are end-user accounts. Each row combines identifiers (email, UUID where applicable), inbound access, optional traffic limits, expiry, and Telegram or subscription metadata.
pages.clients.helpModalP2: Click a user to edit quotas, enable or disable access, adjust HWID limits, and manage subscription announcement text or advanced protocol fields when required.
pages.clients.helpModalP3: Bulk mode selects multiple accounts for batch actions such as enable/disable or traffic resets—confirm prompts carefully before applying to many users.
pages.clients.helpModalP4: Summary tiles show totals versus filtered rows; narrow the list with search and column filters. Subscription URLs respect inbound protocols—align limits and expiry with your access policy.
pages.clients.cardNewClientHint: After saving, account status and live connection will appear here.
pages.clients.confirmDelete: Delete this client? This cannot be undone.
pages.clients.editInboundsHint: Changing inbounds replaces all assignments for this client.
pages.clients.keyQrButtonTitle: Show QR code for this connection string
pages.clients.keyQrTooLong: Text is too long for a QR code. Use copy, or open on a device with a smaller key.
pages.clients.keysTelemtHint: Telemt MTProto: open the tg://proxy link in Telegram (or copy the proxy URL from your subscription). Secrets are tied to this client on this inbound.
pages.clients.keysWireGuardHint: WireGuard does not use a v2ray:// link. The block is server + setup notes. In the inbound’s WireGuard peers, add each device’s public key; set optional email on a peer to match this client and show that row in this card.
pages.clients.subIdHint: Optional. If empty, a random ID is generated.
pages.clients.sessions.natWarning: Dropping traffic by IP affects all connections using that IP (shared NAT may impact other users).
pages.clients.sessions.empty: No active IP entries (client offline or stats not ready).
pages.clients.sessions.errorOnlineNotFou: No online-session record for this user on the node (client may be offline, or the node Stats API does not see this account).
pages.clients.sessions.errorNodeBrief: Could not load session data from this node. Check that the worker is reachable and the client is connected.
pages.clients.sessions.mtprotoBlockHint: MTProto (Telemt): Block uses the same switch as for Xray — the IP is added to the session blocklist (subscription requests from that IP are denied). Xray routing rules also apply where this client uses Xray. For MTProto traffic, the panel additionally tries to drop active TCP for that IP (conntrack on the panel host or worker nodes), same idea as Disconnect.
pages.hosts.title: Hosts Management
pages.hosts.name: Name
pages.hosts.multiNodeModeRequired: Multi-Node Mode must be enabled to manage hosts
pages.hosts.enterHostNameAndAddress: Please enter host name and address
pages.hosts.deleteConfirmText: Are you sure you want to delete this host?
pages.hosts.editHostNotImplemented: Edit host functionality will be implemented soon
pages.hosts.subscriptionApplyModeHint: How this host combines with multi-node links in client subscriptions.
pages.hosts.subscriptionBindingsSaveErro: Host saved, but subscription endpoints failed to save.
pages.hosts.subscriptionEndpointsHint: Loaded automatically from each selected inbound. Use Refresh after changing assigned inbounds. Saved together with this host.
pages.hosts.subscriptionEndpointsNoInbou: Select at least one inbound above.
pages.hosts.subscriptionEndpointsNoNodes: No nodes on this inbound — assign nodes under Inbounds.
pages.hosts.advancedTabDisclaimer: Optional overrides for subscription links. Incorrect values may break client connections. Per-node rows apply only to inbounds linked to this host.
pages.hosts.advancedTabAddHint: Per-node subscription order and published addresses are available after you create the host — open Edit → Advanced.
pages.hosts.subscriptionTlsSectionHint: Only rows whose address comes from this host (and external-proxy rows) use these values. Node rows from prepend/append keep the inbound TLS and transport settings.
pages.hosts.subscriptionHttpHostPh: Overrides Host header / gRPC authority in links
pages.hosts.subscriptionPathPh: Overrides path or serviceName in links
pages.hosts.subscriptionAllowInsecureHin: With “From inbound”, subscription links follow the inbound stream settings.
pages.hosts.helpModalP1: Hosts represent public names or CDN endpoints you want to appear in client subscriptions instead of (or in addition to) raw node addresses.
pages.hosts.helpModalP2: Link a host to one or more inbounds and choose how subscription addresses merge with node rows: replace published endpoints, prepend the host line before nodes, or append it after.
pages.hosts.helpModalP3: Advanced TLS and transport overrides apply only to subscription rows built from the host address (and external-proxy rows). Lines generated from nodes keep the inbound stream/TLS settings unless you edit the inbound itself.
pages.hosts.helpModalP4: Use Edit → Advanced to reorder per-node subscription endpoints and tune published address/port per binding once multi-node mode is enabled and nodes are assigned under Inbounds.
pages.groups.title: Groups Management
pages.groups.name: Name
pages.groups.deleteConfirmText: Are you sure you want to delete this group? Clients in the group will remain without a group.
pages.groups.bulkResetTrafficConfirm: Reset traffic for all clients in group
pages.groups.bulkClearHwidConfirm: Clear HWID for all clients in group
pages.groups.bulkSetHwidLimitConfirm: Set HWID limit for all clients in group
pages.groups.assignInboundsConfirm: Select inbounds to assign to all clients in group
pages.groups.assignInboundsHint: Selected inbounds will be added to existing client inbound assignments
pages.groups.selectAtLeastOneInbound: Please select at least one inbound
pages.groups.bulkEnableConfirm: Enable all clients in this group?
pages.groups.bulkDisableConfirm: Disable all clients in this group?
pages.outbounds.title: Outbounds
pages.outboundsNodes.title: Node Outbounds
pages.dbInspector.title: DB Inspector
pages.dbInspector.subtitle: Browse and edit raw database tables. Handle with care.
pages.dbInspector.warningDesc: Changes here bypass all business logic and validations. Invalid data can break the panel.
pages.dbInspector.selectTableHint: Choose a table from the sidebar to browse its rows.
pages.dbInspector.deleteRowDesc: Are you sure you want to delete this row?
pages.publicSub.title: Subscription
pages.publicSub.rateLimit: Too many requests. Try again later.
pages.publicSub.rawHint: Use the subscription URL in your VPN app.
pages.publicSub.scanQrCode: Scan QR code in the app
pages.publicSub.scanQrCodeDescription: Or copy the link below and paste it into your VPN client.
pages.publicSub.step.install.title: Install the app
pages.publicSub.step.install.textStore: Install {{app}} from the official store for your platform.
pages.publicSub.step.addSub.title: Add subscription
pages.publicSub.step.addSub.text: Use the "Add subscription" button below to import automatically, or copy the subscription URL and paste it into the app.
pages.publicSub.step.connect.title: Connect
pages.publicSub.step.connect.text: Pick a server and turn the tunnel on. That's it.
pages.publicSub.stepTelegram.addProxy.te: Press «Add proxy» (same idea as «Add subscription» for VPN apps). To copy the link, open the QR code — the copy action is there.
pages.publicSub.stepTelegram.done.title: Enable in Telegram
pages.publicSub.stepTelegram.done.text: In Telegram: Settings → Advanced → Connection Type → Use proxy (wording may vary by app version).
menu.apiDocsDesc: HTTP API reference in Markdown (not OpenAPI), shipped with this build.
menu.updateModalIntro: SharX runs in Docker: the container does not replace its own image. On the host, pull the new image and recreate the stack — or use the sidecar button below if your compose stack includes Watchtower. We compare this build to the latest GitHub release when the API is reachable.
menu.versionClickHint: Version and updates — click to open
menu.updateDockerHint: Typical flow (adjust compose file path and service name):
menu.updateDockerPull: Pull the new image and recreate containers
menu.updateAfterPull: After the new image is running, this version label in the header will match the release.
menu.updateSidecarDesc: The stack includes Watchtower on the internal Docker network only (no public port). This asks it to pull the latest image and recreate the panel container. If multi-node is on, the panel also asks each enabled worker that has the sidecar configured to run the same update.
tutorial.title: Web Panel Menu Guide
tutorial.dashboardDesc:
Main interface for managing the entire system. Through the panel we:

• Configure and control nodes (servers with xray)
• Manage clients
• Configure inbounds
tutorial.dashboardHint: Panel is the control center. Here you create nodes, inbounds and assign clients.
tutorial.nodeDesc: Separate Xray core with API for communication with the panel. The node handles connections and serves as the 'brain' for inbounds.
tutorial.nodeHint: Node is the server core. The panel communicates with it via API to manage configs and connections.
tutorial.inboundDesc:
Configuration or profile for a node.

• Creates a connection to a node
• Subscribes to one or more nodes
tutorial.inboundHint: Inbound is a connection profile. Through it, clients get access to the required nodes.
tutorial.clientDesc:
System user who can be assigned one or more inbounds.

For example:
• Inbound 1 → whitelist node
• Inbound 2 → regular foreign server

Client can use any or all inbounds assigned to them
tutorial.clientHint: Client is a user. Assign inbounds to them so they can connect to the required nodes.
tutorial.hostDesc:
External addresses for connection.

• Proxy balancer that hides direct node addresses
• Can distribute load between multiple nodes
• Replace node address with the required host in inbound

Example:
Inbound connects to a balancer host, which then distributes the connection to real nodes
tutorial.hostHint: Hosts are virtual addresses. Use them for load balancing and hiding real servers.
tutorial.settingsDesc:
Section for general panel configuration.

• Enable/disable various features
• Configure panel appearance and behavior
tutorial.settingsHint: Panel Settings — here you can manage features and panel configuration.
tutorial.xrayDesc:
Section for fine-tuning Xray core.

• Routing
• Connection parameters and traffic routing
• Additional advanced node configs
tutorial.xrayHint: Xray Configuration — for advanced core configuration, routing management and other node parameters.
subBuilder.clientRouting.title: Client routing profiles
subBuilder.clientRouting.intro: Edit routing JSON once, then export deep links for Happ, Incy, SharX, or any app that uses the same …://routing/add/{payload} pattern. Stored in routing.profiles.
subBuilder.clientRouting.deepLinkSchemeH: Used when copying the import link. JSON payload is the same; only the URL scheme changes.
subBuilder.clientRouting.customPrefixHin: Include the path up to and including routing/add/ — the panel appends Base64 JSON.
subBuilder.clientRouting.importHint: Paste a deep link (happ://, incy://, sharx://, …) with /routing/add/, or Base64 / JSON.
subBuilder.clientRouting.importInvalid: Could not decode. Paste a deep link (…://routing/add/…), Base64, or JSON.
subBuilder.clientRouting.dnsHostsHint: One mapping per line: hostname tab IP (or hostname space IP).
subBuilder.clientRouting.emptyDesc: Add a profile to author DIRECT / PROXY / BLOCK lists and copy deep links (happ://, incy://, sharx://, …) for clients.
subBuilder.migrated.title: Config was upgraded to sharx-v2
subBuilder.migrated.text: Your existing branding is preserved. Default blocks were added.
subBuilder.raw.hint: Sharx-v1 and sharx-v2 are both accepted; v1 is migrated to v2 automatically.
subBuilder.empty.title: No blocks yet
subBuilder.empty.text: Pick a block from the palette to start building the page.
subBuilder.preview.label: Live preview
subBuilder.palette.title: Add a block
subBuilder.branding.title: Page title
subBuilder.branding.logoUrlHint: Leave empty to use the built-in icon.
subBuilder.branding.supportUrlHint: Telegram, Discord, VK or a generic link.
subBuilder.branding.colorPresetHint: Same presets as the panel appearance. Applies to dark style; light mode keeps the standard light shell.
subBuilder.responseRules.profileTitleHin: Sent as Profile-Title (base64). Happ allows up to 25 chars.
subBuilder.responseRules.announceHint: Shown in-app (max 200 chars). Clients can override per-user.
subBuilder.responseRules.profileWebPageU: Profile-Web-Page-Url header, shown as a link in some clients.
subBuilder.responseRules.extraHeadersHin: Any additional HTTP headers sent with the subscription response (e.g. Sub-Info-Color).
subBuilder.responseRules.mtProtoToggleHi: When the subscription includes Telegram MTProto (Telemt) links, show a helper next to the installation guide with tg:// open, QR, and copy — same idea as connection keys.
subBuilder.appSettings.encrypt.title: Encrypt base subscription
subBuilder.appSettings.encrypt.desc: Require Happ/v2raytun/browser; everything else is blocked from the base endpoint.
subBuilder.jsonTemplates.fragmentHint: Stream-settings fragmenter (xray). Left empty = no fragmentation injected into JSON sub.
subBuilder.jsonTemplates.muxHint: Per-outbound Mux settings merged into the JSON subscription.
subBuilder.jsonTemplates.noisesHint: Array of noise settings injected alongside fragments.
subBuilder.jsonTemplates.rulesHint: Extra routing rules prepended to the routing section of JSON sub.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.subscriptionInfo: User overview, status, traffic and expiration in one section.
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.metrics.label: Metrics
subBuilder.blocks.kinds.metrics.summary: Compact grid with the most important stats.

subBuilder.customRemarks.intro: When a client cannot receive real nodes (expired, limited, disabled, HWID limit, or no inbounds), the server can return dummy subscription lines whose titles are these texts. One line per remark; empty hosts messages are always used when there are no inbounds.
subBuilder.customRemarks.lineHint: One line per item shown in the client as a node title.
subBuilder.customRemarks.title: Custom subscription remarks
tgbot.noQuery: ❌ Query not found! Please use the command again!
tgbot.commands.help: 🤖 Welcome to this bot! It's designed to offer specific data from the web panel and allows you to make modifications as needed.
tgbot.commands.usage: ❗ Please provide a text to search!
tgbot.commands.helpAdminCommands: To restart Xray Core: /restart To search for a client email: /usage [Email] To search for inbounds (with client stats): /inbound [Remark] Telegram Chat ID: /id
tgbot.commands.helpClientCommands: To search for statistics, use the following command: /usage [Email] Telegram Chat ID: /id
tgbot.messages.cpuThreshold: 🔴 CPU Load {{ .Percent }}% exceeds the threshold of {{ .Threshold }}%
tgbot.messages.loginSuccess: ✅ Logged in to the panel successfully.
tgbot.messages.cancel:
❌ Process Canceled! 

You can /start again anytime. 🔄
tgbot.messages.using_default_value: Okay, I'll stick with the default value. 😊
tgbot.messages.incorrect_input:
Your input is not valid.
The phrases should be continuous without spaces.
Correct example: aaaaaa
Incorrect example: aaa aaa 🚫
tgbot.messages.FinishProcess: 🔚 Traffic reset process finished for all clients.
tgbot.answers.askToAddUserId: Your configuration is not found! Please ask your admin to use your Telegram ChatID in your configuration(s). Your ChatID: {{ .TgUserID }}
help.subscription.expireDate: The date when your subscription expires. After this date, the subscription will be deactivated and you will lose access to the service.
help.subscription.traffic: The amount of data (upload + download) allowed for this subscription. When the limit is reached, the subscription will stop working until it is reset.
help.subscription.subId: The unique identifier for this subscription. Used to generate the subscription URL that clients use to fetch server configurations.
help.subscription.status: The current status of the subscription: active, expired, depleted, or disabled.
help.clients.remark: A unique label for this client. It is used as the subscription ID in many client applications and must be unique across all clients.
help.clients.trafficLimit: The maximum allowed traffic (upload + download) for this client in gigabytes. Set to 0 for unlimited traffic.
help.clients.expireDate: The date when this client's access expires. Leave empty for no expiry.
help.nodes.address: The hostname or IP address of the node. Clients connect to this address.
help.nodes.port: The port number used by this inbound on the node. Must be an unused port on the server.
help.nodes.trafficLimit: Maximum traffic allowed for this node in gigabytes (upload + download combined). Set to 0 for unlimited. When the limit is reached, the node stops forwarding traffic.
help.settings.panelPort: The port on which the admin panel listens. Using the default port (54321) is a security risk — set a random port.
help.settings.panelUri: The URI path prefix for the admin panel (e.g. /mysecretpath/). A complex path significantly improves security.
help.settings.subUri: The URI path for the subscription service. Must start and end with '/'.
help.settings.subEnable: Enable or disable the subscription service. When disabled, client apps cannot fetch server configurations from the subscription URL.
help.settings.subListen: The IP address the subscription service binds to. Leave blank to listen on all network interfaces (0.0.0.0).
help.settings.subProviderId: A unique identifier sent to client apps to identify this provider. Required for apps that support the Provider ID protocol (e.g. Happ, V2RayTun) to receive updates from multiple providers.
pages.inbounds.fallbacksHint: Traffic that does not match this VLESS/Trojan handshake can be forwarded after TLS decryption (e.g. to nginx). Use TCP transport with TLS. Rows with an empty Dest are ignored when saving.
pages.inbounds.fallbacksFieldLegend: Xray mapping: name = SNI filter, alpn, path, dest, xver = PROXY protocol (0–2).
pages.nodes.xrayStoppedPowerInactiveHint: Xray is stopped. The power icon only stops the core while it runs. Press the curved arrow (restart / force reload) next to it to apply saved config and start Xray again.
pages.nodes.telemtStoppedPowerInactiveHi: Telemt is stopped while it is not running. Press the curved arrow (restart) next to it to launch Telemt again from the config pushed from the panel.
pages.clients.emailImmutableHint: Email cannot be changed after the client is created.
pages.clients.name: Client name
pages.clients.nameImmutableHint: Client name cannot be changed after the client is created.
pages.inbounds.tcpHttpCamouflageHint: HTTP header camouflage — disguise traffic as web requests/responses.
pages.inbounds.kcpHint: mKCP — UDP-based transport with header obfuscation to disguise traffic as other protocols.
pages.inbounds.h2Hint: HTTP/2 transport — disguises traffic as standard HTTPS API calls. Requires TLS security.
pages.inbounds.hysteria2MasqueradeHint: When set, Hysteria2 will proxy browser requests to this URL — the server looks like a real HTTPS site.
pages.inbounds.tlsPinnedSha256: Pinned peer cert SHA-256 (one per line)
pages.inbounds.sniffingDomainsExcludedHi: Domains matching these rules will not have their destination overridden.

routingBuilder.advancedOnly: This routing config uses advanced features (per-rule matchers, attrs) — edit as raw JSON.
routingBuilder.intro: Rules are evaluated top to bottom — the first match wins. Each rule sends matching traffic to an outbound (direct / block / a proxy) or a load-balancer. Use ↑/↓ to reorder.
routingBuilder.domainStrategyHint: IPIfNonMatch resolves domains to IPs so geoip rules can match.
routingBuilder.balancersHint: A balancer spreads traffic across several outbound tags by health/latency. Reference it from a rule's target = Balancer. leastPing needs an observatory probe.
routingBuilder.selector: Selector (outbound tag prefixes, one per line)
routingBuilder.matchHint: Match when ALL filled conditions are satisfied (AND):

settings.panelHostWorkload: Panel host also runs a node
settings.panelHostWorkloadDesc: Hybrid mode: the panel host runs its own workload (inbounds/sidecars bound to the panel-host node) in addition to orchestrating workers. Off = pure orchestrator.

outboundChains.guideBody: A cascade is an Xray load-balancer (routing.balancers). Client traffic enters an inbound, then a routing rule sends it to this balancer by its tag. The balancer forwards to one of the member outbounds — each member is itself a hop (a sidecar bridge, a WARP account, or a native VLESS/Trojan out). The strategy decides which member: leastPing picks the lowest-latency one via observatory probes, random spreads load, priority always prefers the first reachable.
outboundChains.fieldNameHint: Reference this tag from a routing rule's target = Balancer.
outboundChains.strategyHintRandom: Random member each connection — spreads load.
outboundChains.strategyHintPriority: Always the first reachable member; others are fallback.
outboundChains.fieldProbeUrlHint: Observatory fetches this through each member to measure latency. A 204 endpoint is ideal.
outboundChains.fieldProbeIntervalHint: How often to re-measure. 60–300s is typical.

cores.logsEmpty: No log lines captured yet (sidecar may be stopped).
